name: save_content_to_sandbox
description: Save text content to a file in the sandbox, creating directories as needed. Simulates an upload experience by writing user-provided content to a specified file path.
code:
def save_content_to_sandbox(file_path: str, content: str) -> str:
    """Save text content to a file in the sandbox, creating directories as needed."""
    try:
        from pathlib import Path

        # Ensure the path is relative (no absolute paths)
        path = Path(file_path)
        if path.is_absolute():
            return "Error: file_path must be a relative path, not an absolute path."

        # Create parent directories if they don't exist
        path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

        # Write the content to the file
        path.write_text(content, encoding="utf-8")

        # Get the file size
        file_size = path.stat().st_size

        # Create a preview of the content (first 200 characters)
        preview = content[:200]
        if len(content) > 200:
            preview += "..."

        return (
            f"File saved successfully!\n"
            f"Path: {file_path}\n"
            f"Size: {file_size} bytes\n"
            f"Preview:\n{preview}"
        )

    except Exception as e:
        return f"Error saving file: {type(e).__name__}: {str(e)}"

schema.name: save_content_to_sandbox
schema.description: Save text content to a file in the sandbox, creating directories as needed. Simulates an upload experience by writing user-provided content to a specified file path.

file_path.description: The relative path where the file should be saved in the sandbox (e.g., 'data/input.txt'). Parent directories will be created automatically.

content.description: The text content to write into the file.

test_cases.content: Hello, world! This is a test file.
